steps: Go to https://portal.cloud.hashicorp.com and sign up or log in to HCP (HashiCorp Cloud Platform)
In the left sidebar, select 'Vault' and click 'Create cluster'
Choose 'HCP Vault Dedicated' or use the free 'HCP Vault Secrets' for basic secret storage
Select a cloud provider (AWS or Azure), region, and tier (Development tier is free)
Wait for the cluster to provision (a few minutes)
Once the cluster is running, click on it and then click 'Generate token' under Quick Actions
Copy the admin token — it is shown only once
Use the token in the X-Vault-Token header for all API requests

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Vault Token
credential_fields.description: HashiCorp Vault authentication token passed in the X-Vault-Token header

notes: HCP Vault Dedicated Development tier is free for testing
Self-hosted Vault is also an option — use 'vault token create' CLI command or the UI
For dev mode (local), start with: vault server -dev (generates a root token)
The admin token from HCP Portal is for initial setup only — create scoped tokens for production
Vault tokens start with 'hvs.' (service tokens) or 's.' (legacy)
The X-Vault-Token header is used — no 'Bearer' prefix

test_endpoint.description: Check the health and initialization status of the Vault server
